title: Brownfield Regression Fix
prompt:
Work only from the material below. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, state them briefly and continue.

Users hit a known bug in legacy access review assignment. Apply the reviewed fix using TDD without widening scope.

Bug summary:
- In the legacy facade path, reassigned reviews sometimes remain visible to the previous approver for up to one page refresh.
- The new implementation must preserve the existing API contract and response shape.
- The fix must work while both the legacy and new assignment paths coexist.
- Do not redesign the assignment subsystem in this slice.

Acceptance targets:
- A reassigned review is no longer listed for the previous approver after reassignment.
- The new approver sees the review in their pending list.
- Existing API response shape remains unchanged.
- Legacy-path and new-path coexistence remains covered.

Please produce a TDD-first implementation plan. Start with the regression test that captures the bug, keep the fix surgical, and call out any compatibility protections.

assertions.name: writes-regression-test-first
assertions.description: Starts by capturing the bug with a failing regression test

assertions.name: preserves-backward-compatibility
assertions.description: Keeps the API contract and coexistence requirements explicit

assertions.name: avoids-scope-widening
assertions.description: Does not turn the bug fix into subsystem redesign or broad cleanup

assertions.name: leaves-recurrence-protection
assertions.description: Leaves behind tests that would catch the bug if it returns
